claim: Individuals display above-chance accuracy in selecting stimulus locations before the stimulus is deterministically selected, consistent with anomalous retroactive influence.

phenomenon: hierarchical weighting of face vs body cues in social threat assessment
claim: Facial emotional expressions drive approachability judgments much more strongly than body expressions, but this dominance is attenuated when bodies signal direct threat.

phenomenon: implicit substitution of physical warmth for social warmth
claim: People unconsciously seek physical warmth to compensate for loneliness, and brief physical warmth experiences reduce the affiliation needs triggered by rejection.
Odd and even numbers carry implicit gender associations (masculine and feminine respectively) that automatically influence judgments despite conscious unawareness of the effect.
Thinking that advances across conceptual domains produces better mood than thinking restricted to a single domain, independent of information variety.
Hierarchical social structures are easier to process and learn than egalitarian structures, and this ease of processing leads to greater liking and positive evaluation.
